memory.title: Memory Configuration
memory.loadMemoryDescription: Allow the agent to access and use previous conversation memories
memory.preloadMemoryDescription: Load the memory automatically at the beginning of the conversation for better performance
memory.preloadMemoryWarning: Warning: Preloading consumes more tokens, increasing the cost of each conversation.
memory.preloadMemoryToolInfo: If not checked, memory search will be available as a tool for the agent to use when needed.
memory.sharedMemoryInfo: Active memory is shared across all conversations. By default, history is maintained per session.

shortTermMaxMessages.label: Maximum Messages in Short-Term Memory
shortTermMaxMessages.description: Maximum number of messages in short-term memory before compression to medium-term memory. Default: 50, Minimum: 10, Maximum: 500.

compressionInterval.label: Compression Interval for Medium-Term Memory
compressionInterval.description: Compress every N messages into medium-term memory. Default: 10, Minimum: 5, Maximum: 100.

baseConfig.label: Memory Base
baseConfig.configDescription: If no configuration is selected, the agent will use the account's active default configuration.

memory.clearAllDescription: This action will permanently delete all stored memories for this agent, including short-term memories and medium-term summaries. This action cannot be undone.

knowledge.loadKnowledgeDescription: Allow access to documents and knowledge bases for more accurate responses
knowledge.knowledgeTagsDescription: Specify tags to filter relevant knowledge

securityAlert.description: For security reasons, sensitive information (such as tokens, API keys, and passwords) is not displayed after saving. If you need to update this information, please enter it again and save the configuration.

errors.assistantIdRequired: Assistant ID is required for assistant type

providerSelector.title: Integration Provider

providerSelector.label: Provider

behavior.transferToHumanDescription: Allows the agent to transfer the conversation to a human agent when necessary
behavior.useEmojisDescription: Allows the agent to use emojis in responses to make communication more friendly
behavior.allowRemindersDescription: Allows the agent to register reminders and tasks for the user
behavior.allowContactEditDescription: Allows the agent to edit contact information during the conversation
behavior.allowPipelineManipulationDescription: Allows the agent to move conversations between pipelines and stages according to defined rules
behavior.allowProductSales: Allow recording sales on the pipeline
behavior.allowProductSalesDescription: Lets the agent attach sold products to the pipeline card during the conversation
behavior.allowManageLabelsDescription: Allows the agent to add and remove labels on the current conversation
behavior.timezoneDescription: Defines the timezone used by the agent for scheduling and reminders
behavior.sendAsReply: Send message as reply in conversation
behavior.sendAsReplyDescription: Allows the agent to send messages as a reply to a specific message in the conversation

inactivityActions.description: Configure actions that the agent should execute when the client stops responding.
inactivityActions.messagePlaceholder: Ask if the client is still interested

transferRules.description: Configure instructions for the agent to transfer the service.
transferRules.instructionsPlaceholder: When the client wants to talk about such subject...
transferRules.modalDescription: Configure when and how the agent should transfer conversations to humans, other agents, or teams.

pipelineRules.description: Configure instructions for the agent to manipulate pipelines and assign conversations to specific stages.
pipelineRules.instructionsPlaceholder: When the client mentions interest in product X, move to this stage...
pipelineRules.generalInstructions: General instructions (when and what to do):
pipelineRules.generalInstructionsPlaceholder: Define when the agent should create tasks, add services, or perform other actions in this pipeline...
pipelineRules.selectPipelineFirst: Select a pipeline to add stages
pipelineRules.modalDescription: Configure when and how the agent should move conversations between pipelines and stages.

contactEditRules.description: Allows the agent to edit contact information during the conversation. Configure which fields can be changed and when this should happen.
contactEditRules.enableEditingDescription: Enables the agent to modify contact information
contactEditRules.instructionsDescription: Define when and how the agent should edit contact information
contactEditRules.instructionsPlaceholder: Ex: Update the contact name when they introduce themselves. Add email when the customer provides it. Update city when the customer mentions their location...
contactEditRules.tipContent: Be specific about when each field should be edited

pipelineAutomation.description: Configure automations for moving conversations in pipelines and automatic task creation when moving to each stage.
pipelineAutomation.whenToMove: When to move to this stage?
pipelineAutomation.instructionsPlaceholder: Ex: Move to this stage when the customer shows interest in buying...
pipelineAutomation.noStagesConfigured: No stages configured. Add stages to create automations.

knowledge.title: Knowledge Base Configuration
knowledge.subtitle: Select which knowledge base this agent will use to search for knowledge
knowledge.configDescription: If no configuration is selected, the agent will use the account's active default configuration.
knowledge.noConfigsDescription: You need to create at least one active knowledge base configuration. Go to Knowledge Base Configurations to create one.
knowledge.howItWorksDescription: When the agent needs to search for knowledge, it will use the knowledge base selected above. If no specific configuration is chosen, your account's active default configuration will be used. You can manage all configurations in Knowledge Base Configurations.

loadKnowledge.label: Load Knowledge
loadKnowledge.description: Allows the agent to access and use knowledge from the knowledge base

maxResults.label: Maximum Results
maxResults.description: Maximum number of results the agent will return when searching for knowledge. Default: 5, Maximum: 20.

preload.label: Preload Knowledge
preload.description: Automatically loads knowledge at the start of a conversation for better performance
preload.warning: Warning: Preloading consumes more tokens, increasing the cost of each conversation.
preload.toolInfo: If not checked, knowledge search will be available as a tool for the agent to use when needed.

tags.label: Knowledge Tags
tags.description: Select tags to filter which knowledge entries this agent will use. If no tags are selected, the agent will have access to all knowledge.
tags.placeholder: Type a tag and press Enter...

tools.title: Tools
tools.subtitle: Configure available tools for the agent

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.subtitle: Connect your agent to other applications, this allows it to obtain more precise information or schedule meetings for you.

elevenlabs.description: With ElevenLabs you give your agent the ability to respond to your customers in audio, making it even more humanized.

elevenlabs.whenClientAsksInAudio: When the customer asks in audio

googleCalendar.description: With Google Calendar your agent will be able to schedule meetings, create call links, and already send invitations.

knowledgeNexus.description: Lets the agent query the EvoNexus knowledge base (hybrid search) before answering.
knowledgeNexus.intro: Connect this agent to an EvoNexus knowledge space so it can look up curated information before answering.
knowledgeNexus.baseUrlHint: Address of the EvoNexus dashboard (no trailing slash).
knowledgeNexus.apiKeyHint: API key generated in Nexus under Knowledge → API Keys. Format: evo_k_<prefix>.<secret>.
knowledgeNexus.apiKeySaved: ✓ API key already configured — leave blank to keep, retype to replace.
knowledgeNexus.apiKeySavedPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep the saved key
knowledgeNexus.spaceHint: Spaces are fetched from your Nexus instance once URL + API key are filled.
knowledgeNexus.spaceIdHint: UUID of the knowledge space this agent will query.
knowledgeNexus.spacesEmpty: No spaces found for this API key.
knowledgeNexus.spacesError: Could not load spaces. Check the URL and API key, or paste the Space ID manually.
knowledgeNexus.spacesNeedCreds: Enter the base URL and API key first.

wizard.title: Create New AI Agent
wizard.subtitle: Quick setup in a few steps

step1.title: Create a new AI agent
step1.subtitle: Be creative, choose the name your AI agent will use to introduce itself
step1.nameLabel: What is your AI agent's name?
step1.descriptionHelp: A brief description of what the agent does

step2.title: Agent Type
step2.subtitle: Choose the type that best fits your use case

llm.label: LLM (Language Model)
llm.description: Conversational AI — reasons, answers questions and chats using a language model

task.label: Task
task.description: Orchestrates tasks across sub-agents, each with a defined output

sequential.label: Sequential
sequential.description: Runs sub-agents one after another, passing results down the chain

parallel.label: Parallel
parallel.description: Runs multiple sub-agents simultaneously to speed up independent work

loop.label: Loop
loop.description: Repeats sub-agents until a condition is met or iterations complete

external.label: External Integrations
external.description: Delegates to Flowise, n8n, Typebot, Dify or OpenAI workflows

descriptions.sequential: Define the order of agents that will be executed in sequence
descriptions.parallel: Select the agents that will be executed in parallel
descriptions.loop: Choose the agents that will be executed in loop
descriptions.task: Add sub-agents that will help execute the task

step3.optionalNote: Optional - you can skip and configure later

taskConfig.title: Task Configuration
taskConfig.subtitle: Configure the tasks this agent will execute
taskConfig.addTasksHint: Add tasks using the form below
taskConfig.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this task should do...
taskConfig.toolsFromAgents: Tools from selected LLM agents will appear here

step4.title: Role and Goal
step4.subtitle: Define the role and main objective of your agent (optional)
step4.roleHelp: What is the role or function of this agent?
step4.goalPlaceholder: Ex: Resolve customer questions and problems efficiently
step4.goalHelp: What is the main objective this agent should achieve?

step5.title: Agent Instructions
step5.subtitle: Describe how your agent should behave and respond
step5.instructionPlaceholder: Ex: You are a specialized sales assistant. You help customers find products and answer questions about orders. Always be cordial, proactive and focused on providing the best customer experience.
step5.minCharacters: Minimum 10 characters - be specific about expected behavior
step5.validationError: Briefly describe what your agent does (minimum 10 characters)
step5.tipContent: Be clear and specific. The more details you provide, the better the agent will understand its role and respond accordingly.
step5.aiNotConfigured: OpenAI integration not configured. Go to Settings → Integrations to enable AI features.

promptGenerator.title: AI Prompt Generator
promptGenerator.subtitle: Answer the questions below to generate a personalized prompt

contexto.label: 1. What is the Context of the task?
contexto.placeholder: Explain the situation, scenario, problem, main objective or why this is being done.

objetivo.label: 2. What is the exact Objective of the content you want to generate?
objetivo.placeholder: What do you want the model to produce? (e.g.: text, email, script, copy, analysis, code, strategy, etc.)

estilo.label: 3. What is the desired Style?
estilo.placeholder: How do you want the content to be written? (e.g.: professional, technical, simple, storytelling, creative, academic, etc.)

tom.label: 4. What should be the Tone of the response?
tom.placeholder: Emotional and communicative tone (e.g.: friendly, formal, inspiring, direct, motivational, light humor...)

audiencia.label: 5. Who is the Audience?
audiencia.placeholder: Who will this content be directed to? (e.g.: customers, students, programmers, leaders, HR, general public...)

formato.label: 6. What should be the Response Format?
formato.placeholder: How should the result come? (e.g.: list, continuous text, table, code, steps, bullets, ready final structure...)

exemplo.label: 7. Is there any example (few-shot) you want to use as reference?
exemplo.placeholder: If yes, paste here (text, model, script, excerpt...).

regras.label: 8. Are there specific rules the model must follow?
regras.placeholder: (e.g.: avoid certain terms, follow guidelines, maintain word limit, use tags, etc.)

pontosCriticos.label: 9. Is there any critical point that cannot be missing?
pontosCriticos.placeholder: Mandatory items, important observations, hard restrictions.

instrucoesExtras.label: 10. Do you want to include any extra instructions?
instrucoesExtras.placeholder: Any additional detail that didn't fit in the previous questions.

messages.fillAtLeastOne: Fill at least one field to generate the prompt
messages.fillPromptToReview: Please enter a prompt to review

step6.title: AI Model
step6.subtitle: Choose which artificial intelligence will power your agent

success.title: AI agent created successfully!
